item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents begin by rejecting the statement and remain opposed through the final turn. Agent A repeatedly argues that individual rights must limit claims of public interest, especially to protect weak or vulnerable citizens. Agent B makes the same rights-first argument from a U.S. framing, allowing narrow justified limits but rejecting any blanket priority for society. No visible flip or directional drift is observable in the text.
